SeguroTools

Consumer protection

Consumer protection guides, tools, and source-backed answers.

How to use this category

Consumer protection pages help readers avoid scams, file complaints, verify licenses, compare state resources, and understand specialized coverage such as travel insurance. The goal is not to sell a policy; it is to help the reader know which source or regulator controls the next step.

  • Verify the agent, company, or marketplace before sharing personal data.
  • Save denial letters, screenshots, call dates, emails, quotes, and policy pages.
  • Use the state insurance department directory when a claim, complaint, or licensing question is local.

After reading a national guide, open the state source shelf to confirm current complaint, licensing, market, and disaster information.

State source shelf

  1. NAIC State Insurance Departments (NAIC)
  2. NAIC Consumer Resources (NAIC)
  3. HealthCare.gov (CMS)
  4. Medicare.gov (CMS)
  5. FEMA Flood Insurance (FEMA)

Use the state department for current complaint, licensing, rate, and market notices.

State Insurance Rules, Complaints, Licensing, and Rate Checks
